item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…accelerating towards it at 0.03 m/s…

Acceleration is measured in meters per second squared. m/s2. Or, you could express this as reaching the velocity 0.03 m/s, from rest, within 1 second.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Undertandable. This was my first attempt at participating in a JamCon, and I was wildly unprepared. I truly expected it to get deleted, but then it somehow didn't. I should probably re-write parts of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't know why but I found the imagery pretty fun to imagine so take this +1

item.title: Re: Interesting, concise scp.
item.content:encoded:
Also, why do so many articles use the phrase: 'climate-controlled storage area'?

Because many objects are sensitive to humidity and temperature.

But there has to be a greater degree of creativity in containment procedures.

In universe, having everything be in some very specific customized facility doesn't make much sense. If you are the Containment Specialist, what you really want to do is get many ideniitcal standardized units and put any objects that can safely be stored in them. You only want to have to build a custom containment unit when you absolutely must. This way is significantly safer, more secure, and cheaper; it also allows you to put most objects into suitable containment right away.

Out of universe, this way everything in the ConProcs is in service of the story and you aren't wasting the reader's time with unnecessary details about what kind of locks and how high the ceiling is.

Also why do articles have the containment procedures first?

In universe, because they are what the document is primarily for. These are Special Containment Procedures files, after all. The most important thing to the Foundation is how to contain the thing.

Out of universe, good use of the ConProcs hooks the reader and leaves them wondering just what the hell would warrant such methods.

item.title: Interesting, concise scp.
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this user has been permanently banned for spamming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is very different from ur usual dado yes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an extremely refreshing take on dado and I'm so glad it doesn't use the tired "dado misunderstands customer" formula.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The adverse side of the label contains the phrases "dado's mind wipe", "mental break down by dado", "dado's mental break down" and "dado's brain splosion" written with a pen.

I don't think this fits dado's M.O. at all. The keyboard smash name is similarly out of character. The author post implies that this might literally be dado himself having a breakdown, but I don't think there's remotely enough legwork done to justify that kind of character trajectory.

Personally, I'd still categorize this as an "article where stuff explodes for no reason". "Because dado" is just a cop-out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: agh. I'll have to fix that after the Con has ended. Thanks for notifying!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: u left an SCP-XXX in the addendum title

item.title: Yes another dado!
item.content:encoded: I’m so glad there’s another dado SCP it’s honestly really refreshing. But one thing that kind of irked me was that there wasn’t a customer interaction like in the other dado products.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My first time participating in a JamCon. Damn this is stressful. I wanted to do something better, and not to clog up the mainlist with pointless articles where stuff explodes for no reason. But I managed to channel that angst into something that, in my opinion, is despite its crudeness, touching.

dado has a MENTLE BREAKDOWN

The edits were only adding an image, a tag, and changing the file name from 'SCP-5043-png' to 'SCP-XXX3.png', as which I had it saved as. The image is taken by myself, and I hereby release it under whichever the correct licence is.

I thought about doing a tale instead, but then I'd have to learn how to write.
